     5|import type { Metadata } from "next"
     6|import content from "@/content/es.json"
     7|
     8|const c = content as any
     9|const staticProducts = c.home?.productCatalog?.products || []
    10|const base = "https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com"
    11|
    12|function slugify(s: string) {
    13|  return s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9áéíóúñü]+/g, "-").replace(/-+$/, "")
    14|}
    15|
    16|export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }): Promise<Metadata> {
    17|  const { slug } = await params
    18|
    19|  // Try DB first, fall back to static content
    20|  let name = ""
    21|  let description = ""
    22|  let imageUrl = ""
    23|  let price = ""
    24|
    25|  try {
    26|    const supabase = await createClient()
    27|    const { data: allData } = await supabase.from("ej_products").select("name, description, image_url, price")
    28|    if (allData) {
    29|      const match = allData.find((p: any) => slugify(p.name) === slug)
    30|      if (match) {
    31|        name = match.name
    32|        description = match.description?.substring(0, 160) || match.name
    33|        imageUrl = match.image_url
    34|        price = match.price
    35|      }
    36|    }
    37|  } catch {}
    38|
    39|  if (!name) {
    40|    const staticProd = staticProducts.find((p: any) => slugify(p.name) === slug)
    41|    if (staticProd) {
    42|      name = staticProd.name
    43|      description = (staticProd.description || staticProd.name).substring(0, 160)
    44|      imageUrl = staticProd.imageUrl || ""
    45|      price = staticProd.price
    46|    }
    47|  }
    48|
    49|  if (!name) return {}
    50|
    51|  const title = `${name} | El Viajero — Camping, Pesca y Aventura en Paraguay`
    52|  const ogImage = imageUrl || "/images/og-viajero.webp"
    53|
    54|  return {
    55|    title,
    56|    description,
    57|    openGraph: {
    58|      title,
    59|      description,
    60|      url: `${base}/producto/${slug}`,
    61|      type: "website",
    62|      images: [{ url: ogImage, width: 1200, height: 630, alt: name }],
    63|    },
    64|    twitter: {
    65|      card: "summary_large_image",
    66|      title,
    67|      description,
    68|      images: [`${base}${ogImage.startsWith("http") ? "" : "/"}${ogImage}`],
    69|    },
    70|    other: {
    71|      "product:price:amount": price,
    72|      "product:price:currency": "PYG",
    73|    },
    74|  }
    75|}
    76|
    77|export default async function ProductPage({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) {
    78|  const { slug } = await params
    79|  return (
    80|    <ToastProvider>
    81|      <AuthProvider>
    82|        <ProductContent slug={slug} />
    83|      </AuthProvider>
    84|    </ToastProvider>
    85|  )
    86|}
    87|", "total_lines": 86, "file_size": 2547, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

     5|const c = content as any

     9|
    10|export function ProductJsonLd({ productName }: { productName: string }) {
    11|  const product = allProducts.find((p: any) => p.name === productName)
    12|  if (!product) return null
    13|
    14|  const parseGs = (s: string) => parseInt(s.replace(/[^\d]/g, ""), 10) || 0
    15|
    16|  const schema = {
    17|    "@context": "https://schema.org",
    18|    "@type": "Product",
    19|    "name": product.name,
    20|    "description": product.description || "",
    21|    "image": product.imageUrl || "",
    22|    "brand": { "@type": "Brand", "name": product.brand || "El Viajero" },
    23|    "offers": {
    24|      "@type": "Offer",
    25|      "price": (parseGs(product.price) / 7400).toFixed(2),
    26|      "priceCurrency": "USD",
    27|      "priceValidUntil": new Date(Date.now() + 365 * 86400000).toISOString().split("T")[0],
    28|      "availability": product.stock > 0 ? "https://schema.org/InStock" : "https://schema.org/OutOfStock",
    29|      "url": "https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/producto/" + slugify(product.name),
    30|    },
    31|  }
    32|
    33|  return (
    34|    <script
    35|      type="application/ld+json"
    36|      dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(schema) }}
    37|    />
    38|  )
    39|}
    40|
    41|export function BreadcrumbJsonLd({ items }: { items: { name: string; url: string }[] }) {
    42|  const schema = {
    43|    "@context": "https://schema.org",
    44|    "@type": "BreadcrumbList",
    45|    "itemListElement": items.map((item, i) => ({
    46|      "@type": "ListItem",
    47|      "position": i + 1,
    48|      "name": item.name,
    49|      "item": "https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com" + item.url,
    50|    })),
    51|  }
    52|  return (
    53|    <script
    54|      type="application/ld+json"
    55|      dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(schema) }}
    56|    />
    57|  )
    58|}

Good, I have a clear picture now. Let me assess each area:

1. **Product detail page** - The current page is actually decent but has issues:
   - Hardcoded emoji trust badges (🚚, 🛡, 💬) instead of SVG icons
   - The related products use inline Link cards instead of the new ProductCard component
   - No price USD display
   - The JSON-LD schema has hardcoded price in USD divided by 7400
   - The breadcrumb category link is not clickable
   - Could benefit from a cleaner layout

2. **Cart sidebar** - Currently:
   - Uses hardcoded colors (`bg-white`, `text-[#25d366]`) instead of theme tokens
   - Item cards are basic, could use thumbnails
   - No "continue shopping" link in empty state
   - Missing item images
   - Basic quantity controls

3. **SEO / JSON-LD** - The current setup:
   - Has inline `<script>` tags in product-content
   - The json-ld.tsx component exists but seems unused
   - Schema uses USD instead of PYG
   - Missing aggregateRating, review, sku

Let me now work on all of these. I'll start with the product detail page improvements, then cart sidebar, then SEO, then build and deploy.

Let me also check the BackInStockForm and ProductReviews and other components that might need cleanup.

    43|    .single()

    49|    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Ya existe un post con ese slug" }, { status: 400 })
    50|  }

    52|  posts.push({
    53|    ...parsed.data,
    54|    created_at: parsed.data.created_at || new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0],

    67|  if (authError) return authError
    68|  const supabase = createAdminClient()

    96|  
    97|  const { error } = await supabase

    99|    .upsert({ key: KEY, value: posts }, { onConflict: "key" })

   109|  const slug = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get("slug")

{"content": "     1|#!/bin/bash
     2|set -euo pipefail
     3|cd "$(dirname "$0")"
     4|set -a
     5|source ./.env
     6|set +a
     7|
     8|VERSION=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
     9|DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)
    10|TAG="elviajero:prod-$VERSION-$DATE"
    11|LATEST="elviajero:prod"
    12|
    13|echo "--- build: $TAG"
    14|npm run build
    15|
    16|echo "--- docker: $TAG"
    17|docker build \
    18|  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL="$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL" \
    19|  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" \
    20|  -t "$TAG" -t "$LATEST" .
    21|
    22|echo "--- deploy: elviajero_web (rolling update)"
    23|docker service update --image "$TAG" elviajero_web
    24|
    25|echo "--- done: $TAG"

{"content": "     1|import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"
     2|import { createAdminClient } from "@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/admin"
     3|import { requireAdmin } from "@/lib/auth"
     4|import { BlogPostSchema } from "@/lib/validation"
     5|
     6|export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {
     7|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
     8|  if (authError) return authError
     9|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    10|  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url)
    11|  const slug = searchParams.get("slug")
    12|
    13|  if (slug) {
    14|    const { data, error } = await supabase.from("ej_blog_posts").select("*").eq("slug", slug).single()
    15|    if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    16|    return NextResponse.json(data)
    17|  }
    18|
    19|  const { data, error } = await supabase
    20|    .from("ej_blog_posts")
    21|    .select("*")
    22|    .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
    23|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    24|  return NextResponse.json(data ?? [])
    25|}
    26|
    27|export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
    28|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
    29|  if (authError) return authError
    30|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    31|  const body = await req.json()
    32|
    33|  const parsed = BlogPostSchema.safeParse(body)
    34|  if (!parsed.success) {
    35|    return NextResponse.json({ error: parsed.error.errors.map(e => e.message).join(", ") }, { status: 400 })
    36|  }
    37|
    38|  const { data, error } = await supabase
    39|    .from("ej_blog_posts")
    40|    .insert({ ...parsed.data, created_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    41|    .select()
    42|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    43|  return NextResponse.json(data?.[0] ?? null)
    44|}
    45|
    46|export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {
    47|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
    48|  if (authError) return authError
    49|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    50|  const body = await req.json()
    51|  const { id, ...updates } = body
    52|  if (!id) return NextResponse.json({ error: "id required" }, { status: 400 })
    53|
    54|  const parsed = BlogPostSchema.partial().safeParse(updates)
    55|  if (!parsed.success) {
    56|    return NextResponse.json({ error: parsed.error.errors.map(e => e.message).join(", ") }, { status: 400 })
    57|  }
    58|
    59|  const { data, error } = await supabase
    60|    .from("ej_blog_posts")
    61|    .update({ ...parsed.data, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    62|    .eq("id", id)
    63|    .select()
    64|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    65|  return NextResponse.json(data?.[0] ?? null)
    66|}
    67|
    68|export async function DELETE(req: NextRequest) {
    69|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
    70|  if (authError) return authError
    71|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    72|  const id = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get("id")
    73|  if (!id) return NextResponse.json({ error: "id required" }, { status: 400 })
    74|
    75|  const { error } = await supabase.from("ej_blog_posts").delete().eq("id", id)
    76|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    77|  return NextResponse.json({ success: true })
    78|}
    79|", "total_lines": 78, "file_size": 2991, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}
